content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
(916) 322-5662 
Natalie E. West 
City Attorney 
City of Berkeley 
2180 Milvia Street 
Berkeley, CA 
94704 
Dear Ms. West: 
Administration 
Executive/legal 
322-5901 
Enforcement 
322-6441 
Statements of Economic Interes 
322-6444 
322-5660 
September 30, 1982 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
No. A-82-162 
Thank you for your letter of September 20, 1982, requesting 
advice from this office on the disclosure obligations of the 
members of Berkeley's Board of Adjustments. 
My understanding of the situation is as follows. 
The City 
of Berkeley has a two planning commission system as authorized 
by Government Code Section 65100(b) .ll The Berkeley Planning 
Commission does long-term planning for Berkeley, and the Board 
of Adjustments has the responsibility fo~ zoning matters. 
Members of the Planning Commission have been filing Statements 
of Economic Interests pursuant to Section 87200. 
Members of the 
Board of Adjustments were included in the recently adopted 
Conflict of Interest Code and assigned Article-II type 
disclosure responsibilities. 
You asked whether Berkeley could continue this system for 
disclosure by its planning commissioners. 
Section 87200 requires all city planning commissioners to 
file Statements of Economic Interests pursuant to the 
II All statutory references are to the Government Code. 


Natalie E. West 
September 30, 1982 
Page 2 
requirements of Sections 87202, et~. The Commission is the 
filing officer for Article II filers, including all planning 
commissioners. 
As you know, it was determined that the members 
of all planning commissions organized under Section 65100 are 
"planning commissioners" within the meaning of Section 87200. 
Accordingly, it seems clear that the members of Berkeley's Board 
of Adjustments must file as Article II filers with the 
Commission. 
The Act does not allow the Commission or the 
Commission staff to make exceptions to Section 87200. 
Since the members of the Board of Adjustments have not 
previously filed as Article II filers and were not aware of 
their obligation to do so, we will not require annual statements 
for 1980 and 1981. 
However, the following needs to be done. 
The Berkeley City Clerk should forward the Commission a list of 
the members of the Board of Adjustments no later than 
October 15, 1982; she should also provide each member with a 
Form 721 for filing an assuming office statement pursuant to 
Section 87202. 
This statement should include all investments 
and interests in real property held by the filer on January 1, 
1982, or on the date the filer assumed office, whichever is 
later. 
These statements should be filed no later than 
November 1, 1982, with the city clerk who should make copies of 
each statement and forward the original statements to the 
Commission. 
Annual statements for the members of the Board of 
Adjustments should be filed by April 1, 1983, and should cover 
the entire 1982 calendar year unless the member assumed office 
after January 1, 1982. 
I trust that this discussion answers your concerns. 
If you 
would like to discuss this further, please feel free to contact 
me at (916) 322-5901. 
DMF:plh 
Very truly yours,

LEGAL DEPARTMENT 
2180 MIL VIA STREET 
BERKELEY, CALIFORNIA 94704 
September 20, 1982 
Ms. Diane Maura Fishburn 
Counsel, Legal Division 
City of Berkeley 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Dear Ms. Fishburn: 
(415) 644-6380 
This letter seeks written advice from the Fair Political Practices 
Commission concerning your letter of April 16, 1982, and our subsequent 
telephone conversation of September 20, 1982. 
The City of Berkeley 
has a two planning commission system as authorized by Government Code 
Section 65100 (b). 
Our planning commission deals with long term plan-· 
ning concerns, and our board of adjustments has zoning responsibilities. 
At the present time, members of the planning commission file as Article 2 
filers. 
Members of the board of adjustments are covered by the City's 
Conflict of Interest Code. 
Their disclosure category is equivalent to 
Article 2, but they file on the 730 series of forms, and the forms are 
filed with the City Clerk. 
We would prefer to continue this system since it is already in place 
and members of both commissions are disclosing all information which 
would be required to be disclosed under Section 87200. 
Does the Fair Political Practices Commission take the position that we 
should change our current procedure so that members of the board of ad-
justments file on form 720 and 721 and file directly with the FPPC? 
If 
so, please advise me, and I will take appropriate steps to change our 
current procedures. 
Thank you for your attention to this matter. 
Very truly yours, 
NATALIE E. WEST 
City Attorney 
NEW:gl 
cc: 
City Clerk

sections.question: We would prefer to continue this system since it is already in place and members of both commissions are disclosing all information which would be required to be disclosed under Section 87200. Does the Fair Political Practices Commission take the position that we should change our current procedure so that members of the board of adjustments file on form 720 and 721 and file directly with the FPPC?
sections.conclusion: Section 87200 requires all city planning commissioners to file Statements of Economic Interests pursuant to the requirements of Sections 87202, et seq. The Commission is the filing officer for Article II filers, including all planning commissioners. As you know, it was determined that the members of all planning commissions organized under Section 65100 are "planning commissioners" within the meaning of Section 87200. Accordingly, it seems clear that the members of Berkeley's Board of Adjustments must file as Article II filers with the Commission. The Act does not allow the Commission or the Commission staff to make exceptions to Section 87200. Since the members of the Board of Adjustments have not previously filed as Article II filers and were not aware of their obligation to do so, we will not require annual statements for 1980 and 1981. However, the following needs to be done. The Berkeley City Clerk should forward the Commission a list of the members of the Board of Adjustments no later than October 15, 1982; she should also provide each member with a Form 721 for filing an assuming office statement pursuant to Section 87202. This statement should include all investments and interests in real property held by the filer on January 1, 1982, or on the date the filer assumed office, whichever is later. These statements should be filed no later than November 1, 1982, with the city clerk who should make copies of each statement and forward the original statements to the Commission. Annual statements for the members of the Board of Adjustments should be filed by April 1, 1983, and should cover the entire 1982 calendar year unless the member assumed office after January 1, 1982.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the City of Berkeley may continue its current disclosure system where Board of Adjustments members file using the 730 series forms with the City Clerk, rather than filing Form 720 and 721 directly with the FPPC.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Board of Adjustments members are planning commissioners under Section 87200 and must file Article II statements (Form 720/721) directly with the FPPC, not through the City Clerk using 730 series forms. The FPPC will waive annual statement requirements for 1980-1981 but requires assuming office statements by November 1, 1982 and annual statements by April 1, 1983.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (September 20, 1982 from City Attorney West) and the FPPC response letter (September 30, 1982 from Counsel Fishburn). Extraction focuses on the FPPC response portion. No traditional 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' section headers present, but Q/A content is clearly evident. High confidence due to clear structure and explicit statutory citations.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: We would prefer to continue this system since it is already in place and members of both commissions are disclosing all information which would be required to be disclosed under Section 87200. Does the Fair Political Practices Commission take the position that we should change our current procedure so that members of the board of adjustments file on form 720 and 721 and file directly with the FPPC?

CONCLUSION: Section 87200 requires all city planning commissioners to file Statements of Economic Interests pursuant to the requirements of Sections 87202, et seq. The Commission is the filing officer for Article II filers, including all planning commissioners. As you know, it was determined that the members of all planning commissions organized under Section 65100 are "planning commissioners" within the meaning of Section 87200. Accordingly, it seems clear that the members of Berkeley's Board of Adjustments must file as Article II filers with the Commission. The Act does not allow the Commission or the Commission staff to make exceptions to Section 87200. Since the members of the Board of Adjustments have not previously filed as Article II filers and were not aware of their obligation to do so, we will not require annual statements for 1980 and 1981. However, the following needs to be done. The Berkeley City Clerk should forward the Commission a list of the members of the Board of Adjustments no later than October 15, 1982; she should also provide each member with a Form 721 for filing an assuming office statement pursuant to Section 87202. This statement should include all investments and interests in real property held by the filer on January 1, 1982, or on the date the filer assumed office, whichever is later. These statements should be filed no later than November 1, 1982, with the city clerk who should make copies of each statement and forward the original statements to the Commission. Annual statements for the members of the Board of Adjustments should be filed by April 1, 1983, and should cover the entire 1982 calendar year unless the member assumed office after January 1, 1982.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that Berkeley's Board of Adjustments members must comply with Section 87200 by filing Form 720/721 statements directly with the FPPC as Article II filers, rather than using the local 730 series forms. The FPPC provides a transition schedule and waives prior year penalties given the prior lack of awareness.
